zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Fitz Maurell於1482年出生在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾洛利時，她的父親Thomas Meverell38歲，母親Jane Eyre36歲。她於1495年與John  Thomas Brooke在英國英格蘭結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1514年在英國英格蘭爾麗頓 白 內斯頓享年32歲，埋葬在英國爾麗頓 白 內斯頓。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Fitz Maurell于1482年出生在英国英格兰斯塔福爾洛利时，她的父亲Thomas Meverell38岁，母亲Jane Eyre36岁。她于1495年与John  Thomas Brooke在英国英格兰结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1514年在英国英格兰爾麗頓 白 內斯頓享年32岁，埋葬在英国爾麗頓 白 內斯頓。

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Fitz Maurell was born in 1482, in Throwley, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Thomas Meverell, was 38 and her mother, Jane Eyre, was 36. She married John  Thomas Brooke in 1495, in England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1514, in Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 32, and was buried in Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Fitz Maurell est née en 1482, à Throwley, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Thomas Meverell, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Jane Eyre, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé John  Thomas Brooke en 1495, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1514, à Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 32 ans, et a été enterrée à Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, Angleterre.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Fitz Maurell è nata il nel 1482 nel Throwley, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Thomas Meverell, aveva 38 anni e sua madre, Jane Eyre, aveva 36 anni. Lei sposato John  Thomas Brooke nel 1495, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. ko.lifeSummary: Jane Fitz Maurell(이)가 1482년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 스로우리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Thomas Meverell(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane Eyre(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1495년 영국 잉글랜드에서 John  Thomas Brooke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1514년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 레이턴 비 네스턴에서 32세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 체실르 레이턴 비 네스턴에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Fitz Maurell nasceu em 1482, em Throwley, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Thomas Meverell, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Jane Eyre, tinha 36 anos. Ela casou-se com John  Thomas Brooke em 1495, em Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1514, em Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 32 anos, e foi sepultada em Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ru.Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, England, U: лиитон би нестон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Fitz Maurell родилась в 1482 г. в местности троули, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Thomas Meverell было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Eyre, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John  Thomas Brooke в 1495 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1514 г. в местности лиитон би нестон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 32 лет и была похоронена в местности лиитон би нестон, чешир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Fitz Maurell nació en 1482, en Throwley, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Thomas Meverell, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Jane Eyre, tenía 36 años. Se casó con John  Thomas Brooke en 1495, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1514, en Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 32 años, y fue sepultada en Leighton by Neston, Cheshire, Inglaterra.
